rag_rules: Generate AGREE annex blocks inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Place assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they reference component type ports
Use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
Use assign only when the left-hand side is an existing output feature or declared auxiliary variable in implementation scope
Place input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees in component type annexes when concrete bounds are available
Write output dependency guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs in component type annexes
generic_rules: Treat each annex agree block as scoped to its owning component declaration
Do not encode AADL timing properties as AGREE expressions unless the model exposes explicit typed ports or constants for them
Use implementation-level assignments to bind subcomponent outputs when needed
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use `assume "label": expression;` and `guarantee "label": expression;` syntax for assumptions and guarantees
Use `eq name : type = expression;` for equations and `const name : type = value;` for constants
Use `assign output = expression;` for implementation-level bindings
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not generate clauses for unresolved AADL property references inside AGREE annexes
